task: Launch-quality advanced task: Adaptive Bitrate Incident QoE Attribution. Reality context: a live-stream launch had QoE complaints across regions and devices. Embedded packet: metric map: metric1=startup_time_s, metric2=rebuffer_pct, metric3=bitrate_drop_pct, metric4=isp_share_pct; records: R1 startup_time_s=13.41 rebuffer_pct=64.6 bitrate_drop_pct=36 isp_share_pct=37.6; R2 startup_time_s=38.12 rebuffer_pct=87 bitrate_drop_pct=7.6 isp_share_pct=82.5; R3 startup_time_s=22.63 rebuffer_pct=22 bitrate_drop_pct=36 isp_share_pct=25.2; R4 startup_time_s=51.79 rebuffer_pct=26.7 bitrate_drop_pct=20.3 isp_share_pct=60.7; limit_startup_time_s=26.93; limit_rebuffer_pct=81.5; limit_bitrate_drop_pct=57.9; decision_budget_usd=205000; sensitivity_shift_pct=5; hard_threshold_record_count=1; removed_context="guidance required separating verified evidence from unresolved findings". Rules and constraints: Fit a simple linear or monotone relationship from the embedded rows only; flag records whose residual exceeds the supplied tolerance or reverses expected direction. Options or recommendations cannot use external measurements, specialized licensed software, or hidden assumptions; all calculations must use only the embedded packet, and any contradictory or under-supported record must be marked needs_review. Required work: attribute incident drivers and mitigation; convert the packet into an auditable evidence table, cite the governing values, and list unresolved assumptions. Deliver abr_qoe_attribution.xlsx + incident_review.md with formulas or executable logic, record-level citations, validation checks, sensitivity notes, and a final recommendation table suitable for expert review.
